description_long: This church is a stone masonry structure with a prominent central steeple rising above a square bell tower. The building features a cross-shaped floor plan with a long nave, transepts, and a chancel, all covered by pitched, slate-tiled roofs. The facade is adorned with tall, narrow arched windows, some of which appear to have subtle colored glass, and the main entrance is set within a recessed archway. The steeple is sharply pointed and covered in patterned stonework, giving the church a striking vertical emphasis. The exterior walls are constructed from light gray stone blocks, and the overall design is symmetrical and robust, with buttresses and simple decorative elements. The church's architectural style is reminiscent of traditional Gothic Revival, with its pointed arches, steep rooflines, and verticality.
description: A large, gray stone church with a tall central steeple, arched windows, and a cross-shaped layout. The structure features pitched slate roofs and Gothic Revival architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, large arched windows, and the central steeple rising above the square bell tower.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long nave, side entrance, and the full height of the steeple with additional windows visible on the bell tower.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back wall with a large arched window and the continuation of the pitched roof and steeple.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the nave, transept, and the main steeple, with more arched windows along the side walls.
